name: electronic_circuit_copper_cable_missing_input_chain
notes: BUG: topology_boundaries derivation chain-walk gap. The participating spec feeder:copper-cable:5:7 depends on a chain of feeder belts heading west along y=7: belt@(7,7) West -> belt@(6,7) West -> belt@(5,7) West. Under pre-fix geometry the equivalent was at y=7 with a belt at (3,7) receiving from (4,7) from (5,7). When the region bbox grows to include the immediate feeder (e.g. includes (5,7) but not (6,7)), the east-edge input boundary for copper-cable vanishes entirely instead of chaining one step further to (6,7). SAT then solves the zone with an output at (3,10) or equivalent but no registered copper-cable input -- producing belts that carry copper-cable from thin air. No validation fires because the walker only checks path continuity of EXISTING routed paths; a missing input boundary is invisible to it. Original state: cost=26, 10 entities. After the topology-walk chain-head emit fix (issue #297, branch fix/297-trunk-perimeter-input-emit): cost varies in the 28-30 range across machines (varisat's search is mildly non-deterministic; local runs see 28, CI saw 30). max_cost set to 35 to absorb the variance with a 5-unit margin; if the actual cost climbs past 35, that's a real regression worth investigating. The SAT solution now sources copper-cable through a different path that no longer materialises an in-zone entity at (5,7). The chain is functionally re-connected (production-science-pack, the original repro for #297, validates clean), but the specific (5,7) tile is no longer load-bearing, so the required_entities check on (5,7) was relaxed. (6,7) is retained because it pins the east-edge input the original bug was deleting. Update the fixture again if a future fix routes copper-cable back through (5,7).
